name: Black Mesa
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS: Windows® 10 or Newer
  • Processor: 2.6 Dual Core Processor or Greater
  • Memory: 6 GB RAM
  • Graphics: 2 GB Dedicated Video Card or Greater
  • DirectX: Version 9.0c
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 20 GB available space
comment1: I don't agree with the idea that this game is better than Half-Life 1 and should be played instead of it. Half-Life 1 is a considered a masterpiece for a reason and should be experienced under its own terms, especially because this game does make changes that I believe diminish the experience. Several sections of the game have been made artificially longer by, for example, making you find a valve to screw in to open a door instead of just having a button do the same thing. It's more akin to Half-Life 2's map design in a sense, but it doesn't improve the experience by being there. That being said, Black Mesa is a great game and is worth experiencing as well, but just play Half-Life 1 first. It's impossible to appreciate the changes CC made when developing the game without the proper context.
comment2: Freaking loved every single second of it I've played it over 60 hours or so but the hours won't show up cuz I mostly played it offline,had a hell of a time with my brother playing all the chapters Just wish there was more To this blessing
comment3: A welcome remaster of a classic. An adrenaline rush of an adventure.
